steps.title: Match the present process
steps.content: The sentence describes a general process in the present tense: atoms travel faster and faster and then are diverted. The verb in the "until" clause should use the same simple present pattern.

steps.title: Reject tense shifts
steps.content: A shifts to future tense. C shifts to past perfect. D uses present progressive, which suggests an ongoing action instead of the general condition at which diversion occurs. The answer is B.
